title: entandrophragma caudatum
intro: Entandrophragma caudatum, also known as the cabinetmaker or rosewood tree, is a species of plant that belongs to the family Meliaceae and is native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial activities. The health benefits of Entandrophragma caudatum include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and skin infections. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or topical applications, and it can also be used as a wood source for traditional crafts and furniture-making.
